candidates.why: Explicit top-line revenue measure; labeled as total operating revenue and appears as the primary sales/revenue line.

variables.notes: Uses the clearly labeled top-line revenue row.

candidates.why: Could include both direct and overhead personnel costs; label is too broad to confidently treat entirely as COGS.
Generic operating overhead bucket, likely includes SG&A-type costs rather than pure cost of sales.
By definition SG&A overhead, not COGS.
Appears to be an additional operating expense bucket, likely overhead.
Most conceptually aligned with cost of goods sold / cost of providing services; represents direct costs tied to fulfilling revenue contracts.

variables.notes: The provided candidate list does not include an explicit cost of sales line. 'Cost of contract fulfillment' best matches direct production/service costs (COGS), so it is selected from outside the candidate list. Please confirm this mapping. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Cost of contract fulfillment

candidates.why: Explicit SG&A line; by rule, when present and populated it should be used as the SG&A bucket.
Likely contains both SG&A and possibly production personnel; less precise than explicit SG&A label.
Broad operating expense bucket that may mix SG&A and other costs; secondary to explicit SG&A line.
Another operating expense bucket, but explicit SG&A line is preferable.
Income, not expense; should not be treated as SG&A.

variables.notes: Explicit SG&A line is present and populated, so it alone is used for XSGA_COMPONENTS per the hierarchy rules.

candidates.why: Represents an R&D-related asset on the balance sheet, not a current-period research & development expense line.

variables.notes: No explicit research & development EXPENSE line appears in the income statement; the only R&D-related item is a balance sheet asset. XRD is therefore treated as missing (0).

candidates.why: Primary interest expense line; negative values indicate cost of debt.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Specific component of interest expense related to lease liabilities.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease-related interest expense; used as a fallback if primary lines are missing.
Balancing row tied to interest expenses; used only if main interest expense rows are unavailable.
Interest income, not expense, so not suitable for XINT.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Ordered as a priority list: general interest expense first, then lease-specific and balancing/supplemental lines as fallbacks.

candidates.why: Represents total shareholders’ equity; no separate parent vs non-controlling interest lines are present, so this is the best proxy for book equity attributable to owners.
Subset of equity focused on paid-in capital; excludes other equity components, so less complete than Total Equity.
Component of equity (retained earnings/other reserves), not the total equity figure.
Appears to be a detailed component of other equity, not total equity.
Balancing row, not an economic equity measure.
Balancing item, not the primary reported total.
Represents the balance sheet total, not equity alone.

variables.notes: No separate non-controlling interest line exists, so Total Equity is used for BE and MIB is set to 0.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income before Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Income-statement measure, not a balance sheet minority interest position; cannot serve as MIB (equity).

variables.notes: There is no balance sheet line for minority/non-controlling interests; MIB is therefore set to 0 (empty final_choice).
